Code fragment: public final TableField<AddressEntityRecord, UUID> ADDRESS_ID = createField(DSL.name("address_id"), SQLDataType.UUID.nullable(false).defaultValue(DSL.field("uuid_generate_v4()", SQLDataType.UUID)), this, "");
Explanation: The table has a synthetic primary key (ADDRESS_ID) even though there's already a unique constraint on USER_ID which could serve as a natural primary key. Since each address belongs to exactly one user (one-to-one relationship indicated by the unique constraint), consider using USER_ID as the primary key instead of introducing a separate surrogate key.

Code fragment: public final TableField<OrderProductRecord, UUID> ORDER_PRODUCT_ID = createField(DSL.name("order_product_id"), SQLDataType.UUID.nullable(false).defaultValue(DSL.field("uuid_generate_v4()", SQLDataType.UUID)), this, "");
Explanation: This junction table has a synthetic primary key (ORDER_PRODUCT_ID) even though a composite natural key of (ORDER_ID, PRODUCT_ID) would be more appropriate since it uniquely identifies the relationship between orders and products. Consider using a composite primary key on the two foreign key columns instead of introducing a surrogate key.

Using `fields()` selects all columns from the ADDRESS_ENTITY table, which fetches unnecessary data and couples the code to the table schema. Explicitly list only the required columns in the select statement.
